title: Adani Ports Scores 69, Adani Green Gets 74 in NSE ESG Ratings - HDFC Sky
content:
Adani Ports Scores 69 and Adani Green Secures 74 in Independent ESG Ratings by NSE Sustainability
By HDFC SKY | Last Modified: Jun 9, 2025 12:26 AM IST
Ahmedabad, June 8, 2025 — Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd (NSE: ADANIPORTS, BSE: 532921) and Adani Green Energy Ltd (NSE: ADANIGREEN, BSE: 541450) have disclosed their latest Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) ratings, assigned independently by NSE Sustainability Ratings & Analytics Limited. These assessments were based entirely on publicly available information, as neither company engaged the rating agency for this evaluation.
ESG Ratings Snapshot
The ESG ratings highlight each company’s relative performance on environmental stewardship, social responsibility, and governance practices. While Adani Green received a stronger score of 74, indicating a higher benchmark in ESG performance, Adani Ports followed with a respectable 69, reflecting solid progress across ESG dimensions.
Regulatory Context
Both disclosures were made pursuant to Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, in conjunction with SEBI Master Circular SEBI/HO/CFD/PoD2/CIR/P/0155 dated November 11, 2024.
These ratings were received on June 6, 2025, and formally communicated to the stock exchanges on June 7, 2025, through letters signed by company secretaries Kamlesh Bhagia (APSEZ) and Pragnesh Darji (AGEL) respectively.
About the Companies
Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd is India’s largest private multi-port operator, listed on both NSE and BSE. Headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat, the company develops and manages port infrastructure and logistics services.
Adani Green Energy Ltd is a leading renewable energy company in India, also listed on NSE and BSE. It focuses on solar and wind power generation with a rapidly growing clean energy portfolio aligned with India’s green energy targets.
